Attorney Emily Nestor-Lippert:
I had an exceptional experience working with Emily Nestor-Lippert on my estate planning. Her attention to detail, compassion, and commitment to protecting my family’s future truly set them apart. I left feeling confident that my wishes were properly documented and that my loved ones will be cared for exactly as I intend.Emily really focuses on the Importance of Estate Planning, Estate planning is one of the most meaningful steps you can take to safeguard your family’s future. A well-crafted plan ensures your assets are distributed according to your wishes, reduces stress and confusion for loved ones, and can help minimize taxes and legal complications.
Beyond distributing property, estate planning allows you to appoint guardians for children, outline healthcare preferences, protect family businesses, and provide clarity during difficult times. Whether your estate is simple or complex, having a thoughtful plan in place gives peace of mind—both for you and for those you care about.

I highly recommend Emily Nestor-Lippert to anyone looking for a trustworthy and skilled estate planning attorney

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for posting this. We take it seriously—and we’re not going to dismiss what you’re saying.

First, a Michigan workers’ comp case taking a long time is not automatically a lack of effort. Many delays are caused by the insurance company and defense counsel—disputes, IMEs, medical authorization games, and litigation timelines. That said, you should never feel ignored, uninformed, or like your case is drifting. If that’s your experience, we need to address it directly.

Because of confidentiality, we won’t argue case facts here. But we will do this: review your file immediately, confirm the current posture, and give you a clear, written action plan for what happens next and when. If there has been a communication breakdown, we will fix it. If something needs to move faster, we will push it harder.

Regarding your comments about the office: we handle a high-volume litigation docket, and case files are managed securely. Still, if anything you saw gave you the impression your case wasn’t being handled professionally, that matters—and we want to hear exactly what happened.

Here’s the bottom line: we fight insurance companies for a living. We do not work for them, and we do not take our clients for granted. If you’re still a client, we want the chance to make this right.

Please call our office today and ask for an immediate escalation to management, or email us with your name and best contact number so we can locate your file and schedule a call. We will respond directly and quickly.

This review does not accurately describe what happened with our office.

Our call records (including recordings maintained by our marketing provider) show that you briefly contacted our firm at a time when the attorney was out of the country.

You were informed that he was unavailable for two days. You then ended the call and did not leave a message or provide any information that would allow us to return your call. There were no further contacts.

In addition, you were seeking help with a wrongful discharge matter.

Our firm does not handle wrongful discharge cases, so we would not have been able to accept your case even if we had spoken directly. We focus our practice on workers’ compensation matters so we can provide the best possible service in that specific area of law.

Your accusations that we “lie,” “scam” people, or sell anyone’s personal information are simply false.

We did not request, collect, or use your Social Security number or any other sensitive personal information.

We do not sell or share client information with third parties.

Our positive reviews come from actual clients we have represented over many years, not from “friends and family” posting fake reviews.

We take our reputation and client trust very seriously. While we understand that it can be frustrating when a lawyer is not immediately available or does not handle the type of case you have, that does not justify making inaccurate public accusations.

If you believe there is additional information we should consider, you are welcome to contact our office directly and ask to speak with me so we can address your concerns in a factual and respectful way.

Since then, I've had to have reconstructive surgery on the bones that were broken and on my rotater cuff. In the state of Michigan you have 2 years to file a malpractice lawsuit but there are exceptions which is the 6 year statute of repose but have to show proof that the surgery that was done caused the injury, which I have all the proof needed. On Jakeways' website, it specifically says : Exceptions to the Statute of Limitations

There are some exceptions to the statute of limitations. For example, you may have up to six years to file a claim if the injury involves the reproductive system or the medical provider fraudulently concealed the malpractice. Our Grand Blanc medical malpractice lawyers can advise you about how the law applies to your case.

I've heard nothing but good things about this law firm but in my opinion if you have a medical malpractice lawsuit more than 2 years, look somewhere's else.

Julia contacted me after I had already decided not to use their services to discuss my case further. During that call, I agreed to change a court date but ultimately chose to keep the original date without representation due to the potential risks. There was never any mention that discussing these changes would result in extra charges.

What’s most frustrating is that I was referred to this firm by a previous client who had positive experiences, which makes this situation even more disappointing. I never visited their office or met them in person, yet it seems they charge for every word exchanged. It’s sad and unprofessional to see lawyers prioritizing profit over transparency and trust.

I’d advise anyone considering this firm to confirm all fees and potential charges upfront. Make sure you understand exactly what will be billed, because apparently, even speaking to a receptionist could end up costing you.

Darren, I'm sorry that you took offense to my decision to no longer represent you in your Social Security disability matter. But as we discussed at the beginning of representation, there were certain things that you needed to accomplish in a short matter of time. You were unable to get those things accomplished, so I had to make the decision prior to requesting a hearing, whether to proceed on your matter or decline representation at that point. Since you hadn't been able to accomplish those things, I had to simply make a business decision on whether I thought we could be successful. I have found that clients need to be able to get the medical treatment consistently prior to the hearing to have any chance of being successful in front of the ALJ. Also, I did reach out to you first, and that was to advise you I no longer wished to represent you. I received the decision (dated 11/18/2024) on 11/22/2024, and emailed you the next day on 11/23/2024. We wish you the very best going forward.
